meta.description: High-signal AI/ML people and labs to monitor for frontier intelligence. Safe ingestion only — no login automation, no restricted scraping.

people.name: Andrej Karpathy
people.relevance_to_course: Direct — Neural Networks Zero to Hero maps exactly to Month 1–3 foundations
people.notes: Zero to Hero series is must-watch. Watch one video per week during commute.

people.name: Andrew Ng
people.relevance_to_course: ML Specialization is core Month 2 reference. Short courses useful from Month 1.
people.notes: The AI career advice posts are particularly useful for 70/20/10 framing.

people.name: Sebastian Raschka
people.relevance_to_course: 'Build an LLM from Scratch' is Month 3 anchor reference. Substack newsletter is excellent for Month 2+ ML intuition.
people.notes: Newsletter 'AI Research Insights' is excellent for applied ML. Free Substack tier is sufficient.

people.name: Chip Huyen
people.relevance_to_course: AI Engineering book is the roadmap for Month 3+ production work. Blog posts relevant from Month 2.
people.notes: Her framework for ML systems thinking is directly applicable to this curriculum.

people.name: Lilian Weng
people.relevance_to_course: Blog posts are deeply technical deep dives — Month 4+ reference material for transformers, attention, agents.
people.notes: Extremely dense technical content. File posts in LATER.md until Month 3–4.

people.name: Simon Willison
people.relevance_to_course: Practical LLM engineering — Month 2 reference. API patterns and Python tooling directly relevant.
people.notes: Posts are practical and short. Good commuter reading. Very high signal-to-noise.

people.name: Jeremy Howard
people.relevance_to_course: Practical Deep Learning for Coders is Month 3 reference. Top-down approach complements bottom-up foundations.
people.notes: Part 1 of practical DL is excellent after Month 2 NumPy/pandas foundations.

people.name: François Chollet
people.relevance_to_course: Deep Learning with Python is Month 3+ reference. ARC-AGI work is frontier-only for now.
people.notes: More philosophical/research-focused. Good for frontier 10% allocation.

people.name: Sam Altman
people.relevance_to_course: Industry direction only — 10% frontier allocation. Product roadmap signals matter more than technical details.
people.notes: Low frequency. Most posts are industry commentary. Note product direction changes that affect API access.

people.name: Dario Amodei
people.relevance_to_course: Safety and alignment context for 10% frontier. Technical model papers are relevant.
people.notes: Anthropic's model cards and research are more useful than personal posts.

people.name: Ilya Sutskever
people.relevance_to_course: Research direction signals only. Very rare posts, mostly about AGI timelines.
people.notes: Minimal signal for current phase. Monitor for major research releases.

people.name: Yann LeCun
people.relevance_to_course: Historical deep learning context. CNN foundations relevant Month 3+. AGI debate is frontier noise for now.
people.notes: High volume of opinion posts. Filter for technical content only.

people.name: Harrison Chase
people.relevance_to_course: LangChain/LangGraph are Month 4+ tools. Monitor architecture patterns for RAG/agents context.
people.notes: Parked until Month 4. Monitor LangGraph architecture decisions now for future reference.

people.name: Jerry Liu
people.relevance_to_course: LlamaIndex is Month 4+ RAG tool. RAG architecture patterns relevant from Month 2 embeddings.
people.notes: Parked until Month 4. Note RAG pattern innovations for later reference.

people.name: Clément Delangue
people.relevance_to_course: HuggingFace platform relevant from Month 2 embeddings. Monitor new model releases.
people.notes: HF paper of the day is excellent for tracking model progress. Quick scan 2x/week.

people.name: Jim Fan
people.relevance_to_course: Very frontier — robotics/embodied AI is beyond Month 4+ scope. Monitor for general AI direction.
people.notes: Mostly frontier content. Park everything. Only note if directly relevant to LLM foundations.

people.name: Fei-Fei Li
people.relevance_to_course: CS231n is Month 3+ computer vision reference. Policy work is frontier noise for current phase.
people.notes: CS231n materials are excellent for visual foundations (convolution, etc.) — Month 3+.

people.name: Geoffrey Hinton
people.relevance_to_course: Historical backprop context is valuable. Safety/AGI commentary is frontier scan only.
people.notes: Rare posts. High signal when technical. Note for historical ML context.

people.name: Noam Shazeer
people.relevance_to_course: Transformer architecture co-author. Papers are Month 4+ reference. Very low social media presence.
people.notes: Follow via arXiv. Minimal direct social presence. Transformer paper is canonical Month 4 reading.

people.name: Demis Hassabis
people.relevance_to_course: DeepMind research papers relevant Month 3+. Product announcements relevant as frontier scan.
people.notes: Focus on DeepMind research blog, not personal X posts.

labs.name: OpenAI
labs.relevance_to_course: API patterns relevant Month 1–2. Model updates affect engineering choices Month 3+.
labs.notes: Focus on API changes and engineering-relevant blog posts.

labs.name: Anthropic
labs.relevance_to_course: Claude API is the primary LLM used in this Command Center. Updates directly relevant.
labs.notes: MCP protocol changes matter from Month 3+. Claude API updates relevant throughout.

labs.name: Google DeepMind
labs.relevance_to_course: Research papers relevant Month 3+. Gemini API is alternative to Claude for Month 2+ projects.
labs.notes: Filter for engineering-relevant posts, not pure research papers until Month 3.

labs.name: Meta AI
labs.relevance_to_course: Llama models are Month 3+ local LLM reference. PyTorch is foundational for Month 3+.
labs.notes: Llama model releases are immediately relevant when they improve local inference quality.

labs.name: Hugging Face
labs.relevance_to_course: Paper of the day is excellent frontier signal. Embedding models relevant Month 2. Transformers library Month 3+.
labs.notes: HF paper of the day is the best daily AI signal. Already integrated in ingestion pipeline.

labs.name: Microsoft AI
labs.relevance_to_course: Phi models are excellent for local LLM Month 3+. AI Agents for Beginners curriculum is a reference.
labs.notes: MS AI Agents for Beginners is already in resources. Phi model updates are relevant for local LLM week.

labs.name: Stanford AI Lab
labs.relevance_to_course: CS229 and CS231n are Month 2–3+ references. Research papers mostly frontier scan.
labs.notes: CS229 materials are the canonical ML course for Month 2 reference.

labs.name: MIT CSAIL
labs.relevance_to_course: MIT OCW linear algebra (Strang) is primary Month 1 commuter resource. Already in commuter queue.
labs.notes: Gilbert Strang MIT 18.06 is already in the commuter queue and is the most relevant MIT content.

labs.name: DeepLearning.AI
labs.relevance_to_course: 'The Batch' newsletter is excellent weekly signal. Short courses are Month 2–3 applied references.
labs.notes: 'The Batch' newsletter digest is excellent 10-minute weekly read. Subscribe to RSS.
